other_observers: none; I was not the original finder of this bird. It was previously reported on cobirds, apparently having been at this site for many weeks.
description: The bird had a similar size, shape, and posture to a Yellow-rumped Warbler (seen at the same time).  It had a very prominent white iris, with a bold, broad yellow lore. The neck and throat were gray.  It had prominent yellow flanks.  Its back was a dull greenish color, with two white, moderately broad wingbars.  (My field notes did not include any mention of the bill characteristics)Behaviors: The bird was foraging slowly in trees, about 20 feet above the ground.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
